suggestions.title: Progress bar renders bare '%' when progress is undefined
suggestions.description: In the Dashboard view, Maya's and Carlos's worker cards show 'PROGRESS %' with no number — because worker.progress is undefined and React renders undefined as an empty string, leaving just the '%' suffix. The ProgressBar component renders {progress}% unconditionally. Fix: guard the render with `typeof progress === 'number'` before displaying, or default to 0. Also the progress-pct span should be conditionally rendered: `{typeof progress === 'number' && {progress}%}`.

suggestions.title: Whiteboard text clips and stacks vertically on mobile
suggestions.description: On the 375px mobile viewport, the office whiteboard on the left wall renders as a narrow column with text clipped to read 'WHI / BO / AR / D' stacked vertically — completely unreadable. The whiteboard is positioned absolutely using % coordinates designed for desktop. Fix: either hide the whiteboard entirely on narrow viewports with a CSS `@media (max-width: 500px) { .office-whiteboard { display: none } }`, or reposition it (e.g. move it above the office floor on mobile). The vibe info it displays is already shown in the stats bar anyway.

suggestions.title: Activity log 'Last X events' count uses unfiltered total
suggestions.description: In App.jsx line 1162, the label reads `Last {Math.min(activityLog.length, 20)} events`, but `activityLog` here is the UNFILTERED full array. When the user selects 'Errors' or a specific worker filter, the count still shows the total unfiltered number, misleading users. The filtered array is piped into <ActivityLog entries={...} />, so the count should reference the filtered length instead. Fix: extract the filtered array to a const and use its length: `const filteredLog = activityLog.filter(...); <ActivityLog entries={filteredLog} />` then `Last {Math.min(filteredLog.length, 20)} events`.

suggestions.title: Idle workers render twice: wandering AND seated at conference table
suggestions.description: In Office.jsx, `meetingWorkers` is a slice of `idleWorkers` (line 1000). These same workers are then rendered BOTH as wandering `Character` elements (lines 1330-1350) AND as seated avatars in `ConferenceTable` (lines 846-858). A user will see Sofia wandering the floor AND sitting at the table simultaneously. Fix: either exclude meetingWorkers from the wandering render loop (`idleWorkers.filter(w => !meetingWorkers.includes(w))`), or remove the in-table avatar rendering and rely solely on the wandering characters clustering near the table.

suggestions.title: getRelativeTime duplicated between App.jsx and Office.jsx
suggestions.description: App.jsx defines `getRelativeTime()` (line 28) as a module-level utility, but Office.jsx re-implements the exact same logic inline inside the Character component's ghost bubble JSX (lines 626-635). This is a DRY violation — any fix to relative time formatting must be made in two places. Fix: move `getRelativeTime` to `src/utils/time.js` (or `utils/format.js`) and import it in both files. While at it, `formatFullDateTime` and `getTimeGroup` could move there too.

suggestions.title: Dead condition in DESK_SCREEN_SVG lookup always truthy
suggestions.description: In Office.jsx line 1235: `(isWorking || hasError) && (DESK_SCREEN_SVG[occ.worker.id] || true) &&` — the `|| true` makes the parenthesized expression unconditionally true, rendering the entire SVG lookup check dead. This likely was meant to check whether a custom screen SVG exists before rendering the wrapper div, but the fallback generic SVG inside already handles the missing-key case. Fix: remove `|| true`. The condition should simply be `(isWorking || hasError) &&` — or if the intent was to always show something: just remove the DESK_SCREEN_SVG check entirely since the fallback SVG is already there.

suggestions.title: activityLog.filter() called inline per WorkerCard per render
suggestions.description: In App.jsx lines 1112, 1135, 1148, each WorkerCard receives `activityLog.filter(e => e.worker === w.name).slice(-3)` as a prop, computed inline during render. With N workers and M log entries, this is O(N*M) filtering on every parent re-render. The parent re-renders frequently (60s tick, poll intervals, hover state changes). Fix: memoize worker-specific activity slices: `const activityByWorker = useMemo(() => { const map = {}; for (const e of activityLog) { (map[e.worker] ??= []).push(e) }; return map; }, [activityLog])` — then pass `(activityByWorker[w.name] ?? []).slice(-3)`.

suggestions.title: Dashboard worker cards use role emoji instead of custom SVG avatars
suggestions.description:
The Office view has gorgeous custom pixel-art SVG portraits for each character (Bloberto, Carlos, Maya, Dave, Sofia, Luna). But the Dashboard WorkerCard component (line 253) renders only a generic role emoji: `
{getRoleEmoji(worker.role)}
`. This is a jarring inconsistency — you click a character in the office, land on their dashboard card, and it's a gray emoji blob. Fix: replace the role emoji div with ``. The component is already defined and exported from Office.jsx — just move it to a shared file (e.g. src/components/CharacterAvatar.jsx).

suggestions.title: stuckWorkers useMemo stale: 2-hour check never re-evaluates
suggestions.description: In App.jsx line 379, `stuckWorkers` is computed with `useMemo(() => workers.filter(w => ... Date.now() - new Date(w.updated_at) > 7_200_000), [workers])`. The dependency is only `workers` — but `workers` doesn't change while someone is stuck. `Date.now()` is captured at memo creation time, so a worker who hits the 2-hour mark mid-session will never appear in the stuck card until the next data poll refreshes `workers`. Fix: add the `tick` from `useTimeTick` as a dependency, or compute it inside the `useTimeTick` interval. At minimum, document why the staleness is acceptable.

suggestions.title: Missing 'System' filter option in activity log
suggestions.description: The activity log filter bar has: All, Hires, Completions, Errors — but vibe transition events (type: 'system') injected at App.jsx line 876 have no dedicated filter. Users cannot isolate or hide the system noise ('🌡️ Vibe: Slow Day → Crushing It') from actual team activity. On an active day with frequent vibe changes, system events can flood the log. Fix: add a `{ key: 'system', label: '⚙️ System' }` entry to the filter array in the activity filter bar. Low effort, high signal for power users.

suggestions.title: Mobile header wraps into awkward two-row layout
suggestions.description: On 375px viewport, the header-right section (greeting badge, vibe pill, streak icon, sync button, theme toggle) wraps onto two rows with no visual alignment — the greeting row and the vibe/controls row feel disconnected. The greeting text ('Good evening, LEGEND 🔥 · Sunday, Mar 8') alone is ~250px wide. Fix: on mobile, consider hiding the date portion (or moving it to a subtitle), abbreviating the greeting to just the honorific, and ensuring the vibe pill + buttons stay on one row. Alternatively use a flex-wrap with proper gap/min-width constraints.

suggestions.title: Non-interactive ghost characters are keyboard-focusable with no action
suggestions.description: In Office.jsx line 612, every `Character` component has `tabIndex={0}` unconditionally. Ghost characters (OOO roster members) have no `onClick` handler but are still in the tab order. Keyboard users tabbing through the office will land on ghosts and get nothing — no action, no announcement beyond the aria attributes. Fix: set `tabIndex={onClick ? 0 : -1}` to remove non-interactive characters from the tab order, or ensure the ghost hover card can be triggered via keyboard (Enter/Space) as well.

suggestions.title: Tab switching writes to browser history, breaks back button
suggestions.description: Every `setTab` call also does `window.location.hash = 'office'` or `window.location.hash = 'dashboard'`, which pushes a new history entry. Rapidly switching between tabs (or clicking a character in the office view) can bloat the history stack and make the browser back button useless — it just cycles through office/dashboard states instead of navigating away. Fix: replace `window.location.hash = ...` with `history.replaceState(null, '', '#' + tab)` which updates the URL without pushing a history entry.

suggestions.title: No worker search or name filter in dashboard
suggestions.description: The dashboard already has activity log filtering by worker (via the roster filter bar), but the worker cards grid has no search/filter capability. As the team grows beyond 5-6 workers, finding a specific person requires scanning all cards. The tag filter (Design, QA, Bug, etc.) is a partial solution but name-based search is missing. Fix: add a simple text input above the workers grid that filters cards by name or role. A single `useState(searchQuery)` and `workers.filter(w => w.name.includes(query) || w.role.includes(query))` would suffice.

suggestions.title: Footer quote counter math is opaque and off-by-one prone
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1229: `(quoteBonus % (BONUS_TAGLINES.length + 1)) + 1 / (BONUS_TAGLINES.length + 1)` — the `+1` to the modulo accounts for the vibe tagline slot, but it's implicit and fragile. When `showBonus` is false, the counter still increments on click (because quoteBonus increments), making the displayed 'X/9' misleading (showing slot 1 as the vibe tagline but quoteBonus keeps counting). Recommend extracting a clear `totalSlots = BONUS_TAGLINES.length + 1` const and adding a comment explaining the +1 means the vibe-tagline slot.
